zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire於1357年出生在英國爾南特維其時，他的父親Hugh Le Bird47歲，母親Lucy Leeke x47歲。他於1381年與Dorothy Ann De Weavers在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1410年在英國英格蘭爾享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire于1357年出生在英国爾南特維其时，他的父亲Hugh Le Bird47岁，母亲Lucy Leeke x47岁。他于1381年与Dorothy Ann De Weavers在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1410年在英国英格兰爾享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bird
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a young or a small and slender person, from Middle English brid, bird, burd (Old English bird, brid, perhaps also byrd) ‘bird, young bird’, also ‘young man, young woman, child’.

Irish: Anglicized form of a number of Irish names erroneously thought to contain the element éan ‘bird’, in particular Ó hÉinigh (see Heagney ), Ó hÉanna (see Heaney ), Ó hÉanacháin (see Heneghan ), and Mac an Déaghanaigh (see McEneaney ).

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘bird’, as for example German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Vogel , French Loiseau , Czech Ptáček (see Ptacek ) and Pták, Polish Ptak .

variants.name: Kingbird
meanings: Native American (Chippewa; also King Bird): translation into English of the Chippewa personal name Ogimaa-bines(i), meaning literally ‘the bird that is king’ and referring to the mythological bird-like creature thunderbird. See also Bird 4.
History: The progenitor of the large family of the Kingbirds from the Red Lake, MN, was Ogimaa-bines, a musical gifted Chippewa spiritual leader.

variants.name: Byrd
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Bird  .

variants.name: Heaney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Malachy, Nuala, Ronan.
Irish:

Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉanna and Ó hÉinne, ‘descendant of Éanna’, a very common personal name of uncertain meaning. It was borne by various early Christian saints, most notably Saint Éanna of Aran. The name has been erroneously understood as containing the element éan ‘bird’ and as a result the surname was sometimes Anglicized as Bird .

variants.name: Blackbird
meanings: Native American (also Black Bird): translation into English of a personal name such as Lakota Sioux Ziŋtkala Sapa, which is composed of ziŋtkala ‘bird’ and sapa ‘black’. See also Bird 4.
English (Durham): nickname for someone with a dark beard, from Middle English blak ‘black’ + berd ‘beard’ (Old English blæc + beard).

variants.name: Burd
meanings: English: variant of   Bird  .
Jewish (from Poland and Ukraine): nickname for a man with a notable beard, from a southern Yiddish pronunciation of Yiddish bord ‘beard’.

variants.name: Burt
meanings: English (southern): variant of   Bird  ,   Bright  , or   Burd  .
German: Middle High German burt ‘that which is due or proper’, therefore a nickname for someone who has fulfilled his obligations properly.
Jewish (from Poland and Ukraine): variant of   Burd  .

variants.name: Hummingbird
meanings: Native American (Cherokee): translation into English of a personal name based on the Cherokee name walela for a hummingbird. See also Bird 4.

variants.name: Heagney
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉignigh, ‘descendant of Éigneach’, a personal name meaning ‘forceful’. The spelling of the Irish name was often simplified to Ó hÉinigh and then Anglicized as Heaney or ‘translated’ as Bird , the latter because of a perceived similarity to éan ‘bird’.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Cheshire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name for someone from the county of Cheshire in northwestern England, the name of which is early recorded as Cestrescire, from the name of the county seat, Chester , + Old English scīr ‘district, division’.

variants.name: Chester
meanings: English: habitational name from Chester, the county seat of Cheshire, or from any of various smaller places called with this word (as for example Little Chester in Derbyshire, Chester le Street in County Durham, or Chesters in Northumberland), which is from Old English ceaster ‘Roman fort or walled city’ (from Latin castra ‘legionary camp’).

variants.name: Chesser
meanings: English (Middlesex) and Scottish (Aberdeenshire, Midlothian): variant of   Cheshire  .

variants.name: Cheshier
meanings: English: variant of   Cheshire  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Chesshir
meanings: English: variant of   Cheshire  .

variants.name: Chesher
meanings: English: variant of   Cheshire  .

variants.name: Chessor
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire): variant of   Cheshire  .

variants.name: Chessher
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Cheshire  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire was born in 1357, in Nantwich, Cheshire, England, his father, Hugh Le Bird, was 47 and his mother, Lucy Leeke x, was 47. He married Dorothy Ann De Weavers in 1381, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1410, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire est né en 1357, à Nantwich, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Le Bird, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Lucy Leeke x, avait 47 ans. Il a épousé Dorothy Ann De Weavers en 1381, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1410, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire 1357 in Nantwich, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh Le Bird 47 und seine Mutter Lucy Leeke x 47. Er hat 1381 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Dorothy Ann De Weavers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1410 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire è nato il nel 1357 nel Nantwich, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Hugh Le Bird, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Lucy Leeke x, aveva 47 anni. Lui sposato Dorothy Ann De Weavers nel 1381, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1410, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshireが1357年にイギリスチェシュアイアナントウィチで生まれた時，父Hugh Le Birdは47歳，母Lucy Leeke xは47歳であった。1381年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでDorothy Ann De Weaversと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1410年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire(이)가 1357년 영국 체실르 난트위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh Le Bird(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucy Leeke x(은)는 47세였다. 그는 1381년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Dorothy Ann De Weavers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1410년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire nasceu em 1357, em Nantwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Le Bird, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Lucy Leeke x, tinha 47 anos. Ele casou-se com Dorothy Ann De Weavers em 1381, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1410, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire родился в 1357 г. в местности нантвич, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh Le Bird было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucy Leeke x, 47. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothy Ann De Weavers в 1381 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1410 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Hugh le Bird Knight of Nantwich Cheshire nació en 1357, en Nantwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Le Bird, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Lucy Leeke x, tenía 47 años. Se casó con Dorothy Ann De Weavers en 1381, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1410, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años.
